scenarios.acceptance: A clean Qivryn profile opens the native Agent workspace with the primary sidebar and auxiliary bar hidden, an empty native composer visible, and no React chat editor tab.
steps: Launch the packaged app with a clean user-data directory and an empty workspace.
Wait for extension activation and the native Agent provider registration.
Verify that the native Agent pane and composer are visible and keyboard focus can enter the composer.

scenarios.acceptance: Restarting the packaged app restores the last active non-archived Qivryn run in the native Agent pane without opening the React fallback.
steps: Create two native Qivryn sessions and select the older session.
Reload the workbench, then restart the packaged app.
Verify that the selected run, transcript, composer, and active row are restored.

scenarios.acceptance: Pinned and unread state survives workbench reload and process restart, and changes made in the native list are synchronized to durable Qivryn run metadata.
steps: Pin one run and mark a different run unread in the native Agent list.
Reload the workbench and restart the packaged app.
Verify both states in the native list and in the runtime session metadata.

scenarios.acceptance: Open in Agents Window transfers the selected qivryn-agent session resource, opens that same run in the dedicated window, and clears the one-shot handoff state after restoration.
steps: Open a Qivryn run with a distinctive title and transcript.
Invoke Open Agent in Agents Window from the native editor title action.
Verify that the dedicated window opens the same run and accepts a follow-up prompt.

scenarios.acceptance: Navigating between native sessions and moving one session to the Agents Window does not stop other running Qivryn sessions; their status and streamed events continue updating in both windows.
steps: Start at least two independent Qivryn runs.
Move one run to the Agents Window and navigate to another run in the main window.
Verify both runs complete or remain independently cancelable without transcript loss.
